Subject: Important Information Regarding Fake Check Scams
Body:
Recently, there has been an increase in fraudulent emails posing as payroll services from Cornell University. These emails may contain fake checks in the form of PDF attachments. It is important to be cautious and not attempt to deposit these checks.

If you have already interacted with these emails or have any concerns, please refer to the Beware of Job and Internship Scams guide from Career Services for more information on how to protect yourself. Thank you.
original_body: This phish typically originates from non-Cornell email infrastructure and spoofs (or fakes) a Cornell University email address. The sender may appear as "Payroll Services <payroll@cornell.edu>". This email is typically received after a victim has already replied to a student employment scam. The email contains a fake check in the form of a PDF attachment. Do not attempt to deposit the checks and cease all contact with the sender. See  Beware of Job and Internship Scams  from Career Services for more information.

Subject: Important Notice Regarding Phishing Email

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Subject: Remote Intern Assistant Position Available
Body:
Dear Cornell University Students,

I am reaching out to inform you of an opportunity to work as an intern in a remote position with our university. This position is designed to assist students in covering expenses while working on campus. The tasks can be completed at your convenience, requiring just 1 hour per day, 7 hours per week, with a weekly compensation of $350.

This opportunity offers flexibility, allowing you to choose your working hours. Tasks can be completed from home or on campus. We are currently conducting research that requires the assistance of students from all departments of our institution.

If you are interested in this position, please contact Professor [name] via text at (***) ***-**** with your full name, email, department, and year of study. Professor [name] can provide more information on the job description and application requirements.

Please keep in mind that spots are limited, and applicants will be considered on a first-come, first-served basis.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Sincerely,
[name]
Student Services Recruitment Specialist
Cornell State University

FROM THE OFFICE OF JOB PLACEMENT AND STUDENT SERVICES - CORNELL UNIVERSITY

In reference to the above subject

The Cornell State University will be hiring undergraduate students this week as Interns for a remote position. The aim of this position is to help struggling students cover expenses incurred here on campus. Tasks can be carried out at leisure, taking at most 1 hour per day, 7 hours a week with a weekly pay of $350 as compensation for services rendered.

It is a Flexible Opportunity where you will determine your working time. Since this is a "Remote" Position, tasks can be carried out from home or on campus. We are currently working on a Research that would require the services of Students (Graduate/Undergraduate) of any Department of the Institution to Obtain a Remote Position in this Research. Interested students, Kindly contact Professor [name] via text at (***) ***-**** stating your full name, email, department and year of study regarding the job description and further application requirements.

Please Note: Slots are Limited and Consideration will be given on a first come first serve basis.

[name],

The Student Services Recruitment Specialist,

Cornell State University.

The Cornell IT Security Team

Subject: Exciting Remote Research Internship Opportunity at Cornell University
Body:
Sender: dmrice@optonline.net

Dear Students of Cornell University,

We are pleased to inform you about some new remote internship opportunities available through the Office of Job Placement and Student Services. These internships provide valuable research experience along with a stipend.

Interns will work remotely for up to seven hours per week and will receive a stipend of $400 per week. This opportunity is open to students from all departments at Cornell University.

For more information and to apply, please contact Professor Kraig Adler at kraigadler8@gmail.com. When emailing Professor Adler, be sure to include your full name, alternate email address, department, and year of study.

Positions are limited, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible. We are looking forward to reviewing your applications and wish you continued success in your academic endeavors.

Warm regards,
The Office of Job Placement and Student Services
Cornell University.

I wanted to take a moment to express my gratitude for all the hard work and dedication you have shown over the past months. Your commitment to our team has truly been inspiring.

As a token of my appreciation, I am planning a small surprise for some of the staff in the form of gift cards. I kindly ask for your discretion in keeping this a surprise. I am considering gift cards from popular retailers like Visa, Target, or Amex as they are easily accessible. Do you have a preferred store where you can quickly purchase these on my behalf?

I believe this gesture will be well-received by everyone and show our appreciation for their hard work.

Thank you for all that you do.

Kind Regards,
[NAME]
Vice President and CHRO
Cornell University

Sent from my mobile device
original_body:
Hi [name],
It has been an incredible journey these past months, and everyone has worked so hard and shown a level of commitment that inspires me. I must reward the commitment to work and the staff's dedication during this period.

Hence, I am planning a surprise for some of the staff with gift cards, and your confidentiality would be appreciated so as not to ruin the surprise. I am considering gift cards like Visa, Target, or Amex since we have them almost everywhere. What's the closest store you can find to make a purchase quickly on my behalf?

I am sure everyone would appreciate it. And a little gesture like this will go a long way.

Kind Regards,

[NAME]
Vice President and CHRO
Cornell University

Subject: Important Security Notice
Body:
Dear Cornell Community,

Recently, there have been reports of phishing attempts originating from non-Cornell accounts. These emails may appear as coming from "Desktop Control Service" with a suspicious link to a fake Microsoft login page.

Please do not click on any links in these emails or provide any personal information. If you have already entered your credentials on the fake page, we recommend changing your NetID password immediately as a precaution.

If you have any concerns or questions, please contact the IT help desk for assistance.

Thank you for helping to keep our campus safe and secure.

Sincerely,
Cornell IT Department
original_body: This phish typically originates from a non-Cornell account. The sender may appear as "Desktop Control Service <[user]@handmadebyal.com>". The link within directs to a fake Microsoft-branded login page. Do not follow the link nor contact the sender. If you entered your credentials into the page, change your NetID password immediately at https://netid.cornell.edu .

Subject: Student Research Assistant Opportunity at Cornell University
Body:
Dear Students,

We are excited to announce an opportunity for student research assistant positions at Cornell University. This position is remote and flexible, with tasks averaging 1 hour per day, 7 hours per week, and a weekly compensation of $400.

This opportunity aims to assist students in covering expenses while providing valuable research experience. You will have the flexibility to work from home or on campus, and can schedule your tasks at your convenience.

If you are interested, please contact Professor [NAME] at [EMAIL] with your full name, email, department, student ID or net ID, and year of study. Please note that slots are limited and will be filled on a first come, first served basis.

Thank you for considering this opportunity and we look forward to working with you.

Sincerely,
Office of Job Placement and Student Services - Cornell University
original_body:
FROM THE OFFICE OF JOB PLACEMENT AND STUDENT SERVICES -CORNELL UNIVERSITY

In reference to the above subject

CORNELL UNIVERSITY will be hiring students this week as Interns for a remote position.

The aim of this position is to help struggling students cover expenses incurred here on campus.

Tasks can be carried out at leisure, taking at most 1 hour per day, 7 hours a week with a weekly pay of $400 as compensation for services rendered.

It is a Flexible Opportunity where you will determine your working time. Since this is a "Remote" Position, tasks can be carried out from home or on campus. Interested students, Kindly Contact Professor [NAME] via email on [EMAIL], stating your full name, email, department, student ID or net ID and year of study regarding the job description and further application requirements

Please Note: Slots are Limited and Consideration will be given on a first come first served basis.

The Office of Job Placement and Student Services at Cornell University is excited to announce a remote job opportunity for undergraduate students. We are looking to hire interns to help cover expenses while working from home or on campus. This position requires 1 hour per day, 7 hours per week, with a weekly pay of $350.

This is a flexible opportunity where you can choose your working hours. Tasks can be completed at your convenience. We are also conducting research that requires the services of students from any department.

If you are interested, please email Professor [Spoofed Name] at {[username]@gmail.com} with your full name, email, department, and year of study. Please note that slots are limited and will be filled on a first come, first serve basis.

Thank you,

[Spoofed Name]
Student Services Recruitment Specialist
Cornell University

Subject: Administrative Assistant/Coordinator Position Available
Body:
Hello,

The Centre for Disability Services Council (CDS) is currently seeking to fill a full-time administrative assistant/coordinator position, which can start as soon as possible. This opportunity is ideal for individuals completing their registered internship experience or any qualified students looking to gain valuable experience during the academic year.

The position is virtual and does not require any travel, making it easy to combine with other commitments. It entails 6 hours of work per day, 4 times weekly, with a compensation of $620.00. This role involves assisting in purchasing teaching materials for teachers and students in disability centers.

You will be working closely with Dr. [NAME], who recently relocated to Canada, creating an urgent need to fill this position. Dr. [NAME] will personally provide training for the selected candidate.

To apply for this position, please email your details using your alternate email address (different from your school email) to Dr. [NAME] at [email address]. Include your full name, current residential address, alternate email, and cell phone number in your email.

Thank you for considering this opportunity.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Subject: Remote Internship Opportunity for Students
Body:
Dear Undergraduate and Graduate Students,

The Department of History is pleased to offer a remote internship opportunity through the Office of Job Placement and Student Services. This position is open to all interested and qualified students.

Position: Remote Intern or Assistant
Department: All Departments

Qualifications:
- Must be currently enrolled as an undergraduate or graduate student at any Faculty.
- Access to a computer and stable internet connection.

Responsibilities:
- Assist faculty and staff in various departments with research tasks.
- Conduct experiments and literature reviews under their guidance.
- Maintain communication through online platforms and participate in virtual meetings.

Compensation:
Selected students will receive a weekly pay of $800.00, disbursed at the end of each week. Applicants will attend an online orientation session upon selection.

How to Apply:
Please contact the Head of Department in History via WhatsApp at +1 (415) 533-0075 with your full name, year of study, department, and alternate email address. Applications will be considered on a first-come, first-served basis.

We welcome applications from students of all fields, nationalities, and citizenship statuses.

Thank you for considering this opportunity. We look forward to receiving your application.

- Currently enrolled as an undergraduate or graduate student at any Faculty.
- Must have access to a computer and a stable internet connection.

Responsibilities:
- Assist faculty and staff in various departments with research tasks.
- Conduct experiments and literature reviews under their guidance.
- Maintain regular communication through online platforms and actively participate in virtual meetings.

Compensation:
Selected Students will receive a weekly pay of $800.00 disbursed at the end of each week. Upon selection, applicants will receive a notification email and must attend an online orientation session before starting work.

We welcome applications from students of all fields, nationalities, and citizenship statuses.

We hope to receive your applications soon.

Dear [Team/Students],

Mrs. Lu is in the process of moving and is offering several of her late husband's musical instruments and equipment to staff and students at no cost. However, please be aware that shipping fees will apply.

The available items include a Yamaha AV7-44SG Violin, Leica S (TyR 006) Digital SLR Camera, 2014 Steinway & Sons Piano with Foldable Legs, Eric Clapton's Martin OOO-42 Guitar, Mavic Quadcopter Drone, and Roland D-50 Keyboard.

If you are interested, please send a text message to (+1 205 897 9426) with your shipping address, the item(s) you are interested in, and the estimated shipping cost (between $100 and $500, depending on your location). Mrs. Lu will coordinate shipping through her movers, as in-person pickup is not available at this time.

Please remember not to reply to this email. All inquiries and interest should be directed via text to the provided number.

Thank you for your understanding and cooperation.

Best regards.

Subject: Important Information Regarding Job Scams
Body:
Hello,

We wanted to inform you about a common scam that has been circulating through emails. The sender usually uses a non-Cornell account and sends a blank email with a Word document attachment. This attachment often contains a fake job offer from UNICEF. We advise you not to respond to the sender or open the attachment. The name and email address within the attachment may vary.

For more information on how to protect yourself from internship and job scams, please refer to the tips provided in the "Beware of Internship and Job Scams" document from Career Services.

Thank you and stay vigilant.

See Beware of Internship and Job Scams  from Career Services.

Subject: Important Security Update
Body:
Hello [Recipient Name],

I wanted to bring to your attention a common phishing scam that we are seeing. These emails usually come from a non-Cornell address and may appear as if they are from one of our employees, such as "[Spoofed Name] <[user]@gmail.com>". Please do not respond to these emails.

The scammer's objective is to deceive individuals into making fraudulent financial transfers using personal payment tools like Zelle. If you receive any suspicious emails like this, please report them to our IT department immediately.

Thank you for staying vigilant and keeping our systems secure.

Best regards,

[Your Name]

Subject: Long Term Care Information for Cornell University Employees
Body:
Dear Faculty and Staff,

We would like to inform all faculty and staff members about the Long Term Care (LTC) coverage available to all campus employees. If you are not currently enrolled in LTC or have questions about its benefits, we encourage you to take the time to learn more.

LTC is an important aspect of planning for the future that is often overlooked. By planning ahead, you can provide your family with peace of mind knowing that you are prepared.

Thank you for considering this important information.

Sincerely,

NES

NES has been supporting educational employees for over thirty years and understands the unique challenges you face in planning for the future. We are here to help you navigate these challenges.

To unsubscribe, please opt out.

Subject: Administrative Assistant Position Available at Cornell University
Body:
Dear Students,

Cornell University is currently looking to hire an Administrative Assistant for the Cornell Agri-Tech Summer Research Scholars Program. This position is open to both undergraduate and graduate students, and can be filled by work-study or non work-study applicants.

The position is part-time and offers a weekly salary of $350. If you are interested in applying, please send us your full name, contact phone number, and year of study. You can email this information to us or text (***) ***-**** to receive more details about the job responsibilities and application process.

Thank you for considering this opportunity.

Sincerely,

[First Name Last Name]
Recruiting Manager

We wanted to bring to your attention that there have been recent phishing attempts related to fake award grants and skills grants. These emails are not legitimate and do not come from a Cornell account. Please do not respond to the sender.

If you receive an email with an attachment titled "GRANT DETAILS.docx" and no message in the body of the email, please delete it immediately. It is always important to be cautious of suspicious emails and not open any attachments from unknown sources.

Subject: Exciting Opportunities for Research Assistants and Interns at Cornell University (Fully Remote)
Body:
Dear Students,

I am reaching out on behalf of Professor <name> from the Department of Sociology to share an exciting opportunity for students interested in gaining valuable research experience at Cornell University.

Cornell’s Global International Research is dedicated to tackling important social and economic challenges through innovative research. We are a community driven by a mission to create a better future for generations to come.

We are currently offering remote internship and research assistant positions for students who are eager to:

- Gain firsthand experience in academic research
- Collaborate virtually with experienced mentors and peers
- Develop problem-solving skills by working on cutting-edge projects

Participants will receive mentorship and contribute to Cornell’s commitment to excellence, diversity, equity, and inclusion.

Our interdisciplinary teams at Cornell strive to address real-world issues and turn research into practical solutions. We are looking for undergraduate and graduate students from any institution who are passionate about making a difference.

Key details about the position:

- Title: Research Assistant / Remote Intern
- Open to students from all academic backgrounds and citizenship statuses
- Flexible hours and remote work, with a weekly stipend of up to $765.50
- Applicants should email Professor <name> at <personal email> to apply

We are excited to review your application and welcome you to join us in shaping the future through groundbreaking research at Cornell University.

Warm regards,

The Remote Internship & Research Team
Cornell Research & Innovation
222 Day Hall, Ithaca, NY 14853
Cornell’s Global, Cornell University
original_subject: Cornell University Is Calling: Internships & Research Assistant Roles (Fully Remote!)
original_body:
Cornell University Remote Internship & Research Opportunities Dear Students,

On behalf of Professor <name> from the Department of Sociology, I am pleased to share an exceptional Position for students seeking meaningful research experience through Cornell University.

About Cornell's Global International Research Cornell’s Global stands at the forefront of social and economic education, dedicated to addressing the pressing challenges of our time through innovative, purpose-driven research. We are a dynamic community united by a singular mission: to enhance the world for future generations.

Is this an ambitious vision? Certainly. For over a century, Cornell University’s College of Arts & Sciences has been a catalyst for change, and we are committed to continuing this legacy into the future. Join us as we shape a better tomorrow.

Make an Impact from Anywhere Cornell’s Global, in collaboration with the Office of Research, Undergraduate and Graduate Research, and Student Career Services, is offering remote internship and research assistant positions designed for curious, motivated students.

These roles are ideal for individuals who aspire to:

- Gain hands-on experience in academic research
- Collaborate virtually with leading mentors and peers
- Tackle innovative challenges and enhance challenge-solving skills
Participants will receive dedicated mentorship and contribute to Cornell’s commitment to excellence, diversity, equity, and inclusion.

Why Choose Cornell? At Cornell, research extends beyond discovery—it is a catalyst for change. Our interdisciplinary teams pursue bold ideas that address real-world challenges, ranging from local community issues to global concerns.

Innovation That Matters We transform research into reality. Through partnerships with entrepreneurs, public agencies, and industry leaders, Cornell converts ideas into technologies and solutions that shape the future.

Position Overview Position Title: Research Assistant / Remote Intern College: College of Arts & Sciences Departments: Unrestricted participation across all departments and Colleges within Cornell’s Global Eligibility - Open to undergraduate and graduate students from any institution
- Students from outside Cornell are strongly encouraged to apply
- Must have access to a computer and stable internet
- All academic backgrounds and citizenship statuses welcome
- Flexible, remote role with minimal weekly hours; runs year-round with no fixed end date
Key Responsibilities - Assist faculty and staff with research tasks across departments
- Conduct literature reviews and support ongoing experiments
- Join virtual meetings and maintain consistent communication
- Support data collection, analysis, and manuscript preparation
- Contribute to experiment design, recruitment, and database management
Compensation Interns/Research Assistants will receive a weekly stipend of up to $765.50 , paid at the end of each week. Selected candidates will be contacted via email and must complete an online orientation prior to commencement.

How to Apply Email Professor <name>’s (Alternative Application Processing Contact Information) at:
📧 <personal email>

Subject line: Cornell Internship & Research Assistant Application Inquiry

Include the following in your message:

- Full Title (Firstname and Lastname)
- Nationality
- School Title (Institution)
- Year of Study
- Department
- Alternate Email Address
- Telephone Number
- Current Residential Address
Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Due to exclusive availability, we strongly encourage early submission.

We are excited to receive your application and hope you will take this position to contribute to groundbreaking research at Cornell.

Warm regards,
The Remote Internship & Research Team
Cornell Research & Innovation
222 Day Hall, Ithaca, NY 14853
Cornell’s Global, Cornell University

Subject: Student Research Assistant Opportunity at Cornell University
Body:
Dear Cornell Students,

Are you interested in gaining valuable research experience and working with faculty members remotely? Cornell University is currently offering an opportunity for student research assistants from all departments to support ongoing research projects. Participants will receive a weekly stipend of $400.

About the Research:
Our department focuses on addressing important challenges in chemical, biological, and environmental engineering. Research tasks include computational modeling, experimental systems, clinical research, and field studies.

Eligibility & Application:
All currently enrolled Cornell students are welcome to apply. Research tasks are designed to be completed remotely.

To apply, please send an email to Gary Koretzky, Interim Vice Provost for Research, at interimvpr.cornell.edu@gmail.com with the following information:

- Full Name
- Year of Study
- Department
- Alternate Email Address

Please use “Cornell University” as the subject line of your email. Applications will be reviewed on a first-come, first-served basis due to limited availability.

We look forward to receiving your application.

Sincerely,
The Remote Internship Team
Cornell University

Subject: Student Research Employment Opportunity at Cornell University

Best regards,
Department of History
Cornell University

Subject: Important Notice regarding Suspicious Emails
Body:
Hello,

We have noticed an increase in phishing emails from non-Cornell accounts requesting cell phone numbers. These emails may appear to be from a spoofed Cornell email address and impersonate university leadership. The sender usually includes only a short message or signature block. Please do not respond to these emails as they are likely attempts to scam you into purchasing gift cards.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

The sender and message body may vary slightly, but typically always includes only a short message or just a signature block. Do not reply to the sender. The scammer's goal is typically to coerce the victim into purchasing gift cards.

Subject: Remote Internship Opportunity
Body:
Dear Students of Cornell University,

I am excited to share information about some remote internship opportunities available through the Office of Job Placement and Student Services. These internships provide both financial compensation and valuable research experience.

Participants will work a maximum of seven hours per week, completing all tasks remotely. Selected students will receive a stipend of $400 per week. This opportunity is open to students from all departments.

To learn more and apply, please contact Professor Dorit Abusch at doritabusch1@gmail.com. Please include your full name, alternate email address, department, and year of study in your email.

Positions are limited, so we recommend applying as soon as possible. We look forward to receiving your applications and wish you continued success in your academic journey.

Sincerely,
The Office of Job Placement and Student Services
Cornell University.

Subject: Account Extension Offer
Body:
Hello,

We wanted to let you know that you have the opportunity to extend your account for free. Simply click on the link below to take advantage of this offer:

[link]

[link]

We understand that there has been some debate around the viability of single-player AAA games in the gaming industry. Recent events, such as the closure of Visceral Games by Electronic Arts, have raised concerns about the future of narrative-driven games. However, there have been positive developments, such as the success of Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order, which have sparked renewed interest in single-player titles.

While the market may currently lean towards live service games with multiplayer components, there is still a place for high-quality, story-driven single-player experiences. Companies like EA and Xbox Game Studios are recognizing this and are exploring ways to continue developing these types of games. By leveraging services like cloud gaming and subscription models, they are able to reduce the risks associated with AAA-scale budgets.

Thank you for being a valued account holder. We hope you enjoy the benefits of extending your account for free.

Best regards,

eveloped as a single-player experience.

The question of the financial viability of single-player AAA games was raised following the closure of Visceral Games by Electronic Arts (EA) in October 2017. Visceral had been a studio that established itself on a strong narrative single-player focus with Dead Space, and had been working on a single-player, linear narrative Star Wars game at the time of the closure; EA announced following this that they would be taking the game in a different direction, specifically "a broader experience that allows for more variety and player agency". Many commentators felt that EA made the change as they did not have confidence that a studio with an AAA-scale budget could produce a viable single-player game based on the popular Star Wars franchise. Alongside this, as well as relatively poor sales of games in the year prior that were principally AAA single-player games (Resident Evil 7, Prey, Dishonored 2, and Deus Ex: Mankind Divided) against financially successful multiplayer games and those offe
r a games-as-a-service model (Overwatch, Destiny 2, and Star Wars Battlefront 2), were indicators to many that the single-player model for AAA was waning. Manveer Heir, who had left EA after finishing his gameplay design work for Mass Effect Andromeda, acknowledged that the culture within EA was against the development of single-player games, and with Visceral's closure, "that the linear single-player triple-A game at EA is dead for the time being". Bethesda on December 7, 2017, decided to collaborate with Lynda Carter to launch a Public Safety Announcement to save single-player gaming.

A few years later in 2021, EA was reported to have revived interest in single-player games, following the successful launch of Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order in 2020. The company still planned on releasing live service games with multiplayer components, but began evaluating its IP catalog for more single-player titles to revive, such as a remake of the Dead Space franchise. Around the same time, head of Xbox Game Studios Phil Spencer said that they still see a place for narrative-driven single-player games even though the financial drivers of the market tended to be live service games. Spencer said that developing such games with AAA-scale budgets can be risky, but with availability of services like cloud gaming and subscription services, they can gauge audience reaction to these games early on and reduce the risk involved

Subject: Easily Request Files from Others with Dropbox
Body:
Hi Jose,

I wanted to let you know about a new feature on Dropbox that allows you to easily and securely request files from others. With our File Request feature, you can collect content from anyone - even if they don't have a Dropbox account.

Additionally, you can share files with others using view-only access, which allows them to preview the content without being able to make any changes. And if you need to collaborate on content, you can give them edit access to folders or specific files.

If you want to learn more about these new features, click here.

If you no longer wish to receive tips from Dropbox, you can unsubscribe here.

Thanks for using Dropbox,

Dropbox, Inc.
original_subject: Hello jose, you can easily & securely request files from others with Dropbox
original_body:
File sharing just got an upgrade

Any changes made to a shared file will be automatically updated and synced for all recipients.

Click to view your Dropbox

Give others a preview with view-only access

Want someone to be able to see your files but not be able to edit them? Share your content with view-only access.

Collaborate using edit access
Need others to collaborate on content with you? You can give them edit access to entire folders or specific files.

Collect content with a File Request

Dropbox's File Request feature lets you easily & securely request files from others– whether they have a Dropbox account or not.

Dear [Recipient],

I am writing to you on behalf of HSBC London UK Financial Conduct Department. We have been given the authority by the World Bank and International Monetary Fund (IMF) to help clear any outstanding funds that are owed to you, such as Contract fund, Lottery winnings, Inheritance, or Investment. These funds have been held up due to past issues with corrupt Government Officials.

To facilitate the release of your funds in a legal and timely manner, we kindly ask for your cooperation in providing us with your full name, address, and phone numbers. This will ensure that the funds are transferred to you as the rightful recipient without any complications.

Please feel free to reach out to us with any questions or concerns. We are here to assist you through this process.

Yours sincerely,

Mr. Georges Elhedery
HSBC Chief Executive Officer
